title: Reducing the Federal Workforce Through Attrition Act
short_title: Reducing the Federal Workforce Through Attrition Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the federal government to reduce the number of people it employs by ten percent over the next few years. The government will do this by not hiring new workers to replace people who quit or retire. If a government agency has too many employees, they will not be allowed to hire anyone new until they have fewer workers. The goal is to have ninety percent of the current number of federal employees by the year 2028.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Reducing the Federal Workforce Through Attrition Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Reduction in Federal Workforce
sections.text: This section explains how the federal government will reduce the number of people it employs. The word agency means any part of the executive branch of government. The Director means the person who runs the Office of Management and Budget. A Federal employee means someone who works for the government, but does not include people who are appointed by politicians. Starting in fiscal year two thousand twenty eight, the total number of federal employees in all agencies cannot be more than ninety percent of the number working on September thirtieth, two thousand twenty five. Each agency head must report to the Director by October thirty first, two thousand twenty five, showing how many federal employees work for their agency as of September thirtieth, two thousand twenty five. The Director must then tell each agency head by the end of the first quarter of fiscal year two thousand twenty six the maximum number of federal employees that agency can have. From the second quarter of fiscal year two thousand twenty six until September thirtieth, two thousand twenty seven, agencies can only hire one new person for every three people who retire or quit. The Director must watch each agency during this time to make sure they are on track to meet their limits by fiscal year two thousand twenty eight. Starting in fiscal year two thousand twenty eight, the Director must keep checking if agencies have too many employees. If an agency has too many employees, the Director must tell the President and Congress within fourteen days after the end of that quarter. When an agency has too many employees, they cannot hire anyone new, cannot hire people to work from home, cannot let current employees work from home more hours, and cannot give employees more official time for union activities. These restrictions stay in place until the agency gets back under its employee limit. The President can waive these rules if there is a war, national security threat, or emergency that threatens life, health, public safety, or property. When counting employees, agencies must use full time equivalent numbers and cannot be limited by union contracts. The government must make sure it does not hire more contractors just because it has fewer employees, unless those contracts save money. Federal employees can still transfer from one agency to another if the receiving agency is following the rules in this law.
